    <h1 {...{
      "id": "html-events-and-listeners"
    }}>{`HTML Events and Listeners`}</h1>
    <p>{`Handle user interactions using event listeners in JavaScript or inline event handlers`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP supports standard HTML events for handling user interactions like clicks, input changes, and keyboard actions. You can attach event listeners in `}<strong parentName="p">{`JavaScript`}</strong>{` (recommended) or use `}<strong parentName="p">{`inline event handlers`}</strong>{` in HTML (requires additional permissions).`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "prerequisites"
    }}>{`Prerequisites`}</h2>
    <p>{`Before you begin, make sure your development environment uses the following versions:`}</p>
    <ul>

      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong>{` or higher`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Manifest version v5`}</strong>{` or higher`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "example-javascript-event-listeners-recommended"
    }}>{`Example: JavaScript Event Listeners (Recommended)`}</h2>
    <p>{`The recommended approach is to attach event listeners in JavaScript using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addEventListener()`}</inlineCode>{` or by assigning event handler properties.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="HTML, JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<button id="firstButton">Click me</button>
<button id="secondButton">Click me too</button>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Method 1: addEventListener (recommended)
const firstButton = document.getElementById("firstButton");
firstButton.addEventListener("click", handleClick);

// Method 2: Event handler property
const secondButton = document.getElementById("secondButton");
secondButton.onclick = handleClick;

function handleClick(event) {
  console.log(\`Button clicked: \${event.target.id}\`);
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addEventListener()`}</inlineCode>{` is preferred because it allows multiple event listeners on the same element and provides better control over event handling.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "example-inline-event-handlers"

    <p>{`You can define event handlers directly in HTML attributes, but this requires enabling a special permission.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="3" languages="HTML, JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml-1"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<button id="thirdButton" onclick="handleClick(event)">Click me</button>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"

  console.log(\`Button clicked: \${event.target.id}\`);
}
`}</code></pre>

    <p>{`Security consideration`}</p>

});
